shape_semantics: I2S-ANTI. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,_,W: cmplog BLOCKS, while value_profile AND naive RESOLVE (vpc non-decisive). The technique that HURTS is I2S (cmplog's input-to-state substitution): the branch is reached by plain havoc byte/token exploration (naive) and by the gradient (value_profile), but cmplog's extra I2S stage diverts or pollutes the corpus and BLOCKS it. The shape (Layer 1) already asserts I2S-anti; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm cmplog blocks (that would verify the loser-pattern with the loser-pattern, violating G2). It independently discriminates WHY I2S hurts on each branch: (a) active decoy splice that DEPLETES the target byte at a fixed offset; (b) NO harvestable input-resident operand (table/state/property-derived) so I2S is pure overhead; (c) a length/sequence-accumulation gate cmplog SHORTENS away from; (d) an iteration/traversal-DEPTH gate the winner drives DEEPER per seed; (e) a DECODED codepoint reachable in raw byte-space; (f) WHOLE-CORPUS homogenization onto a few CMP-matched byte motifs. ROUND-3 (final): the 6 branches that resisted every scored per-branch and whole-corpus test are honestly closed as decidable:false -- the only built tool not yet tried (token_count) reads ONLY signature.operand_literal, which is null / non-byte / wrong-endian for these gates, so the discriminating decoy/token signal (a magic token in the loser corpus, e.g. 'hasc'/'csah'/'SHOT', '72 10 00') is NOT addressable with current registry plumbing. No mechanism is invented to force a label.

hypotheses.label: I2S splices a WRONG/competing but abundant CMP operand into the input (a well-known font-table tag, a script-tag/codepoint constant, an in-range fixed-point magnitude, or a competing protocol value), pinning the cmplog corpus onto that decoy and DEPLETING it of the target byte value at the winner gate's offset; naive/value_profile never harvest the decoy so they freely reach the target

params.comment: For the branch's W/L gate offset (the divergent byte from the analysis byte-diff / per-role overlay), compute the cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment of the TARGET byte value (the winner-resolving value at that offset) vs the DECOY value (the competing operand I2S substitutes). Anti-direction: cmplog DEPLETES the target (signed_target_enrich < 0) and ENRICHES the decoy (decoy_enrich > 0).
params.target_value_source: signature.operand_literal (e.g. DFontTag 00 00 01 00 for 5391; PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow byte for libpng/3977; 0x3a for openthread/13349,13391; table-tag constants for 5815/5843/5857) at the cmp-site offset from the per-role overlay

hypotheses.prediction: On the gate offset the cmplog corpus is DEPLETED of the target value relative to naive (signed_target_enrich < -0.3) AND/OR ENRICHED in the decoy operand (decoy_enrich > 0): I2S is actively steering bytes AWAY from the winner value. A pure-overhead mechanism (I2S has no operand to substitute) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 and operand_enrichment returning no_gate_signature -- no directional depletion, because there is no decoy to splice. A pro-I2S mechanism would predict signed_target_enrich > 0. Only the active-decoy-substitution mechanism predicts a NEGATIVE target enrichment / positive decoy enrichment, which is what distinguishes 'I2S splices the wrong abundant operand' from 'I2S simply has nothing to do here'.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate needs havoc to BUILD or PRESERVE a long / periodically-repeated glyph-record byte sequence (multi-matra record runs, long multi-glyph Thai state-machine traversal); the gate value is table/state-derived (no harvestable operand), but cmplog's CMP-biased budget produces SHORTER, heterogeneous inputs that never reach the required sequence length, while naive's byte-expansion/copy operators grow and sustain it

params.comment: Reuse joint_necessity's struct-size half: take the median seed SIZE of the winner-resolving (naive/value_profile) seed set vs the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set. size_lift = median(resolving_size) / median(blocking_size). The length/accumulation subtype predicts the resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the blocking seeds. --tokens is unused for this hypothesis (size only); the token-count half of the tool is ignored here.

tokens_by_target.harfbuzz: (size-only; token vocabulary not used for this hypothesis)

hypotheses.prediction: The winner-resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the cmplog-blocking seeds (size_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is driven by sequence/length accumulation that cmplog's CMP-biased, shorter inputs fail to build. A no-operand-overhead mechanism (subtype 3 below) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 -- cmplog and naive reach SIMILARLY-sized inputs and the gate is simply a derived category cmplog can't aim at, with no length asymmetry. A decoy-substitution mechanism would have a fixed-offset operand signature (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This length asymmetry, on a gate with NO harvestable operand, is what separates 'I2S shortens the input below the accumulation threshold' from plain 'I2S overhead on a derived gate'.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate is a loop-exit guard or a deep Ragel state-machine action whose firing depends on how DEEP the input drives the per-buffer traversal (delete_glyphs_inplace backward-cluster loop reaching its false exit; find_syllables_myanmar walking the state machine into a late case action). There is no input-resident CMP operand (operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature) and no whole-input length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis does NOT fire); the distinguishing signal is per-seed iteration DEPTH at the branch line: the winner (naive/value_profile) corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed, while cmplog's CMP-biased budget reaches it far fewer times. I2S overhead here is paid as fewer/shallower traversals, not as a competing operand or a shorter input.

params.comment: Run the winner-resolving vs loser-blocking seed sets through the libafl-harfbuzz-cov image and read the per-seed-normalized execution count of the branch line. depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth. The iteration-depth subtype predicts the winner drives the branch line materially deeper per seed. NOTE (round-2): the two round-1 hint members (5311, 5931) did NOT clear this rule in the campaign (depth_lift < 1.3 or unscorable for lack of >=3 seeds), so they were reassigned away. This hypothesis is KEPT verbatim (confirmed for >=1 branch elsewhere) but no longer hints those two.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed (winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is iteration/traversal depth that cmplog's CMP-substitution budget under-drives, NOT a competing operand (operand_enrichment returned no_gate_signature) and NOT a whole-input length effect (size_lift < 1.3). A pure no-operand-overhead gate predicts depth_lift ~ 1.0. A sequence-accumulation gate would have shown its asymmetry as raw size (size_lift >= 1.3) rather than per-seed depth. This per-seed depth advantage, on a gate with no operand and no length lift, is what separates 'cmplog under-drives the traversal' from 'cmplog just has nothing to substitute'.
hypotheses.rule: skip == 'no_gate_signature' AND size_lift < 1.3 AND winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3

hypotheses.label: The gate is an equality on a value DECODED inside the shaper (a UTF/codepoint slot compared to a small set of constants, e.g. Thai consonants 0x0E1B/0x0E1D/0x0E1F), so I2S harvests the comparison operand but rewrites the WRONG input offsets (the decoded codepoint does not sit at the contiguous input field I2S logs); yet the target value IS reachable in raw input byte-space as a short byte-pair the winner (naive/value_profile) lands by undirected mutation while the loser stalls. Distinct from decoy-substitution because operand_enrichment finds NO directional fixed-offset depletion (the byte does not live at a stable corpus offset), and distinct from pure derived-gate overhead because the operand value IS approachable in byte-space.

params.comment: operand value = the decoded codepoint byte-pair(s) from signature.operand_literal as little-endian byte-pairs (width 2). Slide a 2-byte window over each arm's seeds; per-arm min Hamming distance to the nearest matching byte-pair. Winner (naive/vp) lands the pair; loser (cmplog) stalls. NOTE (round-2): the round-1 hint member harfbuzz/5954 (Thai 0x0E1B/D/F) did NOT clear this rule in the campaign -- the width-2 operand is chance-alignment prone and cmplog ALSO lands 0x0E-high byte-pairs (just a non-matching low byte), so winner_closer was false / distance_gap < 0.15. This hypothesis is KEPT verbatim (confirmed for >=1 branch elsewhere) but no longer hints 5954.
params.width_caveat: operand width 2 is chance-alignment prone (registry caveat); require a non-trivial distance_gap (>= 0.15) so a coincidental near-match does not pass.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner arm lands materially CLOSER to the operand byte-pair than the loser (winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.15): the decoded codepoint IS reachable in raw byte-space and the winner's undirected mutation lands it while cmplog's I2S-substituted wrong offsets stall. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead show a fixed-offset corpus depletion (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature with signed_target_enrich < -0.3). A pure derived-gate-overhead mechanism (no byte-reachable operand at all) would predict winner_closer == false / distance_gap ~ 0. The winner's byte-space proximity advantage to a DECODED operand is exactly what isolates this from both neighbours.

hypotheses.label: I2S COLLAPSES the cmplog corpus's byte-diversity onto a few CMP-matched byte motifs (runs of a magic constant, repeated font-table-tag ASCII fragments like 'hasc'/'csah'/'SHOT', a self-reinforcing low-byte lexical stride like '72 10 00'/'8d 10 00'), so the WHOLE cmplog corpus is BOTH inflated with near-duplicate substitution products AND homogenized in byte composition. The winner gates here are structural / Ragel state-machine actions that require DIVERSE, freely-varied glyph-category byte runs (Myanmar / Khmer category sequences, backward-cluster loop volume); cmplog's homogenized corpus never produces that diversity while naive's undirected havoc does. Measured at the WHOLE-CORPUS level (branch-independent) because these gates have no fixed-offset operand, no byte-reachable literal, no per-seed length or depth asymmetry that any per-branch seed-set tool could get data for -- the signal lives only in cmplog-vs-naive corpus-composition.

params.comment: Whole-corpus, BRANCH-INDEPENDENT comparison of the cmplog saved corpus vs the naive saved corpus for this target (memoized per (target, arm-pair) by the arbiter). winner-fuzzer = cmplog (the arm hypothesized to inflate AND homogenize), loser-fuzzer = naive (the flat baseline). This is the registry's i2s_homogenizes_corpus rule: corpus_size_ratio >= 1.3 (cmplog hoards near-duplicate substitution products) AND composition_entropy_ratio < 0.85 (cmplog byte-composition collapsed onto few motifs). NOTE (round-3): on s4 this scored harfbuzz cmplog-vs-naive but did NOT meet the >=1.3/<0.85 threshold for 5311/5931/5934 in the campaign, so those branches remain unlabeled by it (they fall to the round-3 decidable:false group below). The hypothesis is KEPT verbatim (confirmed for >=1 branch elsewhere).
params.ORCHESTRATOR_WIRING: ALREADY DONE -- bench/arbitrate.py CS_ARMS contains 'i2s_vp_LW_W': ('cmplog', 'naive') (verified present).

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog corpus is BOTH inflated and homogenized relative to naive (corpus_size_ratio >= 1.3 AND composition_entropy_ratio < 0.85): I2S substitution products hoard near-duplicate seeds and collapse byte-diversity, starving the diverse glyph-category runs the winner's state-machine gates need. A 'no corpus effect' alternative (cmplog and naive corpora are similarly sized and similarly diverse, so I2S is merely passive per-execution overhead) predicts corpus_size_ratio ~ 1.0 AND composition_entropy_ratio ~ 1.0 and would NOT fire this rule. A fixed-offset decoy-substitution mechanism would have shown a per-offset depletion (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This whole-corpus inflation+entropy-collapse signature is what separates an ACTIVE corpus-homogenization mechanism from PASSIVE per-execution overhead.

hypotheses.label: The gate value is computed from shaping tables, Unicode-property lookups, Ragel state-machine internals, or derived category classification -- NOT a literal value compared against input bytes anywhere. I2S has no input-resident CMP operand to harvest or substitute; its per-execution callback overhead and misdirected substitution budget crowd out the broad havoc byte exploration that resolves the branch, with NO directional corpus depletion and NO length asymmetry

params.comment: operand_enrichment is asked for a gate signature; on these branches it returns no_gate_signature because the gate is NOT a fixed-offset value comparison (it is a derived/table/state category). The DIAGNOSTIC for this hypothesis is the ABSENCE of a harvestable operand (skip == no_gate_signature) COMBINED WITH the absence of a length asymmetry (the i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation hypothesis, tried first, did NOT fire). This is the residual / catch-all I2S-anti subtype.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand to harvest) AND there is no resolving-vs-blocking length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis is ruled out): I2S simply has nothing to substitute and is pure overhead. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead yield a fixed-offset operand with negative target enrichment (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature, signed_target_enrich < 0). A sequence-accumulation mechanism would yield size_lift >= 1.3. The conjunction no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3 is exactly what isolates 'derived gate, no operand, no length effect -- just I2S overhead' from the two operand/length subtypes.

hypotheses.label: (decidable:false) The gate is a post-shaping / decoded-property predicate whose effect is I2S OVER-SOLVING the comparison -- cmplog harvests the decoded operand and over-commits the corpus to the TRUE side, starving the winner's FALSE-side reach (so cmplog reaches MORE of a specific structure, not less): a multi-codepoint conjunct where cmplog satisfies ZWJ-followed-by-Extended_Pictographic (5723), a post-shaping script enum where cmplog keeps producing Malayalam/Tamil (5881), or a width-2 decoded Thai codepoint where cmplog lands a 0x0E-high byte-pair with the wrong low byte (5954). No BUILT measurement can confirm the over-solve story: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed input offset); value_distance_reached has no usable byte-reachable literal for the script/conjunct cases and FAILED on the width-2 Thai operand (cmplog lands 0x0E-pairs too, distance_gap < 0.15); there is no length asymmetry; the predicate fires at a fixed buffer prefix so depth_reach shows no per-seed depth advantage; and corpus_size_ratio's homogenization signal MISPREDICTS here, because these losers reach a SPECIFIC over-committed structure (true conjunct / a particular script) rather than being starved of diversity. The one untried built tool, token_count, cannot help either: its --literal is driven solely from signature.operand_literal, which is a non-byte script enum for 5881 (unparseable) and a wrong-endian 2-byte codepoint for 5954 (token_count searches 0x0E1B big-endian, the buffer carries 1b 0e little-endian) -- so it returns no parseable literal / a flat ~1 ratio and cannot separate over-solve from generic overhead.

params.comment: These branches compare DECODED/post-shaping values (UTF-decoded codepoints, table-derived script enum, decoded conjuncts) that have no stable input offset and no byte-reachable literal usable by value_distance_reached. The over-solve direction (cmplog reaches the TRUE side / a SPECIFIC script) is the opposite of corpus-homogenization-starvation, so corpus_size_ratio cannot stand in: its entropy-collapse signature would be a FALSE positive here. token_count is also blind (operand_literal non-byte / wrong-endian). No built tool can separate the over-solve story from generic I2S overhead, so they are honestly left non-discriminable.

hypotheses.prediction: No built measurement discriminates the over-solve mechanism from generic I2S overhead here: operand_enrichment == no_gate_signature; value_distance_reached has no usable byte-reachable literal or fails the width-2 chance-alignment floor (5954, distance_gap < 0.15); size_lift < 1.3; depth_lift ~ 1.0; corpus_size_ratio would FALSE-positive (directed, not starved); token_count is blind (non-byte / wrong-endian operand_literal). Honest decidable:false: do NOT force a label.

hypotheses.label: (decidable:false) ROUND-3 honest closure for the three structural/state-machine branches (5311 backward-cluster loop volume; 5931 Myanmar Ragel case-8; 5934 Ra/As/H reph trigraph) whose CARDS describe the LOSER (cmplog) corpus as saturated by a CMP-derived magic token the winner (naive) corpus lacks -- 5931's loser is 'dominated by repeated CMP-derived literals (runs of f9, magic ASCII hasc/csah/SHOT)', 5934's blocking seeds carry 'a repeating 72 10 00 / 8d 10 00 lexical pattern', 5311's cmplog 'spends budget aligning comparison operands rather than growing buffer volume'. The mechanism story (active token-saturation / homogenization that starves the diverse glyph-category runs the winner's gates need) is plausible, but NO BUILT measurement can confirm it for these branches with current registry plumbing: (1) operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset value gate -- the gate is a derived category / loop guard); (2) the per-seed seed-set tools came out flat in earlier rounds (depth_reach depth_lift < 1.3 for 5311/5931; no >=3-seed length asymmetry); (3) the whole-corpus corpus_size_ratio scored harfbuzz cmplog-vs-naive but did NOT meet >=1.3 AND <0.85 in the campaign, so the round-2 homogenization rule did not hold for them; (4) token_count -- the only built tool not yet exercised on this shape -- is driven SOLELY by signature.operand_literal (bench/arbitrate.py line 419), which is null for all three (operand_kind none_structural), so token_count cannot be aimed at the decoy magic token that lives only in the mechanism text. Pointing token_count at that decoy would require a per-branch decoy-literal field the registry/arbiter does not plumb (a NEW measurement, out of scope for the evidence-test author). Per G3 this is an honest non-discriminable group, NOT a forced label.

params.comment: DIAGNOSIS OF NON-DISCRIMINABILITY (not a scorable rule). token_count would, in principle, confirm the saturation story by counting the decoy magic token in naive-resolving (winner) vs cmplog-blocking (loser) seeds: active saturation => literal_presence_ratio = loser_count/winner_count >> 1 (loser enriched in the decoy). But the arbiter drives token_count's --literal ONLY from signature.operand_literal, which is null for 5311/5931/5934 (operand_kind none_structural). The discriminating token (hasc/csah/SHOT for 5931; '72 10 00'/'8d 10 00' for 5934) is in the mechanism_summary, NOT in operand_literal, and is not plumbed as a per-branch param. So token_count returns 'no parseable gate literal' and cannot discriminate. corpus_size_ratio (whole-corpus, branch-independent) already failed to meet threshold for these in the campaign. No remaining built tool can separate active homogenization/saturation from passive I2S overhead for these branches.
params.what_would_decide_it: EITHER a per-branch decoy-literal param plumbed into token_count's --literal (so the magic token from mechanism_summary, not operand_literal, drives the count), with little-endian-aware byte matching; OR a per-call-context coverage tool. Neither is in the current registry.

hypotheses.prediction: No built measurement, as wired, discriminates active corpus-saturation/homogenization from passive I2S overhead for these three branches: token_count is blind because operand_literal is null (the decoy token is not plumbed); corpus_size_ratio did not meet >=1.3 AND <0.85 in the campaign; operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature; depth_reach/length tools came out flat. A token-saturation mechanism (loser >> winner decoy count) and a passive-overhead mechanism (equal counts) are indistinguishable with current plumbing. Honest decidable:false: do NOT force a label.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' is used anywhere -- I2S is untagged in mutation_op. The decoy signature is read from cmplog CORPUS byte frequencies (operand_enrichment); the depth signal from per-seed branch-line execution counts (depth_reach); the value-distance signal from seed byte windows (value_distance_reached); the homogenization signal from whole-corpus byte composition (corpus_size_ratio); and token_count from per-branch seed-set literal counts -- never from a lineage gradient/I2S event.

value_profile is treated as a feedback, not a mutator: its effect is read only via what the resolving corpus REACHES (size, byte-distance, branch-line depth) or via cmplog-vs-naive corpus composition, never via a lineage event.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is used ONLY for the fixed-offset VALUE-gate subtype (decoy-substitution), where W and L seeds align at the gate offset. The sequence-accumulation subtype uses struct_size_ratio (different-SIZED seeds); the iteration-depth subtype uses per-seed depth_reach; the decoded-codepoint subtype uses value_distance_reached sliding windows; the homogenization subtype uses corpus_size_ratio (whole-corpus, no byte-alignment).

corpus_size_ratio is branch-INDEPENDENT (memoized per (target, arm-pair)): one harfbuzz cmplog-vs-naive scan scores every harfbuzz branch routed to the homogenization hypothesis. CS_ARMS already contains 'i2s_vp_LW_W': ('cmplog','naive').

token_count is driven by signature.operand_literal only (bench/arbitrate.py line 419). For the round-3 structural branches operand_literal is null (none_structural) or non-byte / wrong-endian, so token_count cannot be aimed at the decoy magic token that lives in mechanism_summary -- this is precisely why those branches are decidable:false rather than scored by token_count.

On-disk corpus / cov-image dependence: operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, depth_reach, corpus_size_ratio, token_count all need on-disk corpora or the target cov image. For this shape those exist for harfbuzz and openthread on s4. Cross-server: a branch is NOT marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is off-server -- the owning server arbitrates its own targets. The two decidable:false groups here are non-discriminable for a TOOL-CAPABILITY reason (no built measurement can aim at the signal), not a corpus-locality reason.

Seed floor: operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached / depth_reach / token_count need >=3 W and >=3 L seeds. A no_gate_signature or null-metric return caused by <3 seeds is a DATA reason (treat as inconclusive). corpus_size_ratio has no such floor (scans the whole queue).
notes:
ROUND-3 (FINAL) re-design (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET/FREEZE: all SIX prior KEEP hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM in rule and direction (i2s_decoy_substitution_target_depletion, i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation, i2s_overhead_on_iteration_depth_gate, i2s_decoded_codepoint_byte_reachable_gate, i2s_homogenizes_cmplog_corpus, i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand) so the 23 confirmed branches re-validate unchanged -- no rule edited, no relaxed-threshold re-test. The only KEEP edits are member_hint_branches bookkeeping (depth/decoded-codepoint hints that did not validate were emptied).

PRIORS KEPT: 6 (all KEEP-marked, verbatim rules) + the prior decidable:false over-solve group (i2s_oversolve_postshaping_derived_predicate, 5723/5881/5954) carried forward. Decision_order is unchanged (the two decidable:false hypotheses have rule 'false', never match, and are intentionally NOT in decision_order -- they fall to fallback=inconclusive).

NEW MECHANISM (round-3, discriminable): NONE. The only built tool not yet exercised on this shape is token_count. It is GENUINELY DIFFERENT in measurement (per-branch seed-set literal-presence, loser/winner ratio) from every prior tool here, and its mechanism story (cmplog-blocking seeds SATURATED with a CMP-derived magic token the naive-resolving seeds lack -- a per-branch confirmation of the homogenization/decoy-saturation story) is directly supported by the cards for 5931 (hasc/csah/SHOT) and 5934 ('72 10 00'). BUT it is NOT ADDRESSABLE: bench/arbitrate.py drives token_count's --literal solely from signature.operand_literal (line 419), which is null for 5311/5931/5934 (operand_kind none_structural) and non-byte / wrong-endian for 5954/5881. The discriminating token lives in mechanism_summary, not operand_literal, and the arbiter does not plumb a per-branch decoy-literal param. Aiming token_count at it would be a NEW measurement (new param + little-endian-aware matching), which is out of scope for the evidence-test author (round-3 forbids inventing a mechanism / re-plumbing a tool to force a label). I therefore did NOT add a scorable token_count rule -- doing so against operand_literal would silently no-op and falsely route these branches to fallback while implying they were tested.

DECIDABLE:FALSE (G3) -- two honest groups, 6 branches total:
  (1) i2s_oversolve_postshaping_derived_predicate (5723, 5881, 5954): post-shaping OVER-SOLVE losers (cmplog reaches MORE of a directed structure -- the ZWJ+Extended_Pictographic true conjunct for 5723; a SPECIFIC script Malayalam/Tamil for 5881; a 0x0E-high byte-pair with wrong low byte for 5954). corpus_size_ratio's homogenization/starvation signal MISPREDICTS (these losers are directed, not starved); value_distance_reached failed the width-2 chance-alignment floor on 5954; operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature; token_count is blind (operand_literal is a non-byte script enum / wrong-endian codepoint).
  (2) i2s_homogenization_suspected_unaddressable_token (5311, 5931, 5934): cards describe active loser-corpus token-saturation/homogenization, but every built tool either returned flat in earlier rounds (depth_reach, length, corpus_size_ratio did NOT meet threshold) or cannot be aimed at the decoy token (token_count reads operand_literal only, which is null). The mechanism is plausible but non-discriminable with current registry plumbing.

WHY THIS IS THE CORRECT FINAL OUTCOME: rounds 1-3 exhausted the discriminating capacity of the 5 per-branch tools + the 1 whole-corpus tool + the 1 remaining literal-count tool for these 6 branches. The signal that WOULD decide groups (1)/(2) (a per-call-context coverage counter, or a per-branch decoy-literal param fed to token_count with little-endian byte matching) is NOT in the registry. Per G3, an honest decidable:false is the correct outcome; I did not invent a mechanism or relax a threshold to force a label.

OUTCOME SUMMARY: PRIORS KEPT 6 (all KEEP, verbatim rules) + over-solve decidable:false group carried forward. NEW DISCRIMINABLE MECHANISMS 0. DECIDABLE:FALSE 2 groups / 6 branches (5723/5881/5954 over-solve; 5311/5931/5934 unaddressable-token homogenization). The 6 still-unlabeled branches fall to fallback=inconclusive via the two rule:'false' decidable:false hypotheses. No orchestrator wiring is required (CS_ARMS already has the shape; no scorable token_count rule is added because operand_literal cannot address the decoy).
